title: [main.c:192-213] fatal error loop has no watchdog interaction
description:
Completeness: System integration category.

In main.c (lines 192-213), when standalone LoRa init fails, the code enters an infinite loop blinking an LED or sleeping forever.

If CONFIG_WATCHDOG is enabled elsewhere in the system, this infinite loop will trigger a watchdog reset because:
1. The loop never calls any watchdog feed/kick function
2. k_sleep() does not automatically feed the watchdog

This may be intentional (watchdog reset as recovery mechanism), but it's undocumented. If unintentional, the system will repeatedly reset attempting the same failing init.

Options:
1. Document that watchdog is expected to reset the device
2. Explicitly disable watchdog before entering error loop
3. Feed watchdog in the loop if the intent is to stay in error state
close_reason: Fixed: added comment documenting that watchdog reset is intentional for fatal error recovery
